beer.beer_style: IPA - Imperial / Double New England / Hazy

items.checkin_comment: A couple of cheeky ones before I head back to the sanctuary of the beaches . $7 schooners, can’t be that bad

items.comment: Yeah $7 schooners are all right. Beers were good, nothing earth shattering but worth another visit certainly.

items.checkin_comment: Hides the ABV nicely, tropical sharp. Winner

beer.beer_description: A Pro-Am 2025 entry for Sydney Beer Week
beer.beer_style: IPA - Imperial / Double New England / Hazy

items.checkin_comment: Lighter easily drinking. Spice up front, subtle lingering ginger. A sessionable stout? Maybe.

items.badge_description: At a new place and don't want to choose a pint of something you may not like? Grab a few tasters! Enjoy some small pours and then decide which to upgrade to a full pour. That's 295 checkins with the serving style of Taster. Try 5 more for Level 60! Want to learn more about this badge? Check out our podcast

items.checkin_comment: Smooth tropical with a hint of bitterness. Solid Hazy IPA …

items.badge_description: You may not be able to see through the haze, but that juicy, hoppy goodness goes down smooth. What started as a trend is here to stay. That's 430 different beers with the style of IPA - New England / Hazy, Pale Ale - New England / Hazy, IPA - Milkshake, or Pale Ale - Milkshake. Try 5 more for Level 87!

items.checkin_comment: Paddle 2 … that’s a paddling. This goes pretty good, malty and smooth. No RSL carpet flavour … be a good starter or after work beer.
Clubhouse leader from the first paddle.  Passionfruit wheat beer, funky passionfruit taste, but light and sessionable. Probably a 3.90 beer for me.
I agree with my previous review, nice easy drinking brew, possibly more pale than XPA but sessionable.
Ahhh that’s a whole lot better. Zesty, tropical smasher.

items.checkin_comment: After walking the 22km to get here, started with this. Mid strength lager never going to cut it with me.
